stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary function is text generation, utilizing 'safetensors' format.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags, including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Meta's Llama series indicates relevance to the leading edge of large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels, designed for visual generative modeling.
The corpus is highly accessible, featuring 100M training examples and is permissively licensed for both research and commercial use.
GPIC is a curated dataset, having undergone safety filtering and deduplication, and is captioned by a state-of-the-art vision-language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a specific model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, trending on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a generative AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the bigscience/bloom model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies, including transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing local AI inference.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to deploy gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon introduces Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to analyze the hidden pretraining data composition of LLMs.
The research addresses the lack of transparency regarding LLM 'digital DNA' (training data mixture), which dictates model behavior.
The tool estimates the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only generated text output.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel large language model designed to generate editable PCB schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a significant bottleneck in hardware design: the manual and expertise-intensive nature of PCB schematic creation.
The core technical challenge addressed is developing an LLM-suited representation and acquiring a large-scale dataset for this domain.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current LLMs where reasoning is tied to external, autoregressive token generation.
It proposes modeling LLM reasoning using an internal 'working memory' mechanism, inspired by human cognition.
This approach aims to decouple internal computation from external communication, potentially leading to more robust reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a fundamental issue in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global logical consistency.
Introduces a formal metric, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
The metric is designed to be computable at runtime using system output and defined cross-component constraints.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies after static visual encoding.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The work formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus.
LLMSurgeon can perform this estimation using only the text generated by the target LLM, enabling post-hoc auditing.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses the current bottleneck in electronic hardware design, which is largely manual and expertise-intensive.
Overcomes the challenge of lacking LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this domain.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data composition ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The work formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS), a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This research enables post-hoc auditing of LLM data provenance and mixture, which is crucial for understanding model behaviors and failure modes.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a critical gap in hardware design by automating a traditionally manual and expertise-intensive process.
The primary technical hurdles overcome involve creating an LLM-suited representation and developing a large-scale dataset for PCB generation.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation for healthcare by providing a realistic, structured benchmark.
Introduces MedCase-Structured, a pipeline to generate FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured clinical text.
Focuses on enabling controllable evaluation of diagnostic reasoning in Electronic Health Record (EHR) settings.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage tools like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The update facilitates the use of proprietary models (like Claude) alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives (e.g., gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b) for coding tasks.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The botnet's infrastructure was reportedly linked to a residential proxy network based in Russia.
The news falls under cybersecurity, indicating ongoing threats from organized malicious actors.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude Code, Sonnet, Opus) in scientific software development, specifically in physics.
A physicist supervised the AI over 12 days to build a differentiable perturbation theory module (CLAX-PT) in JAX.
The research quantified supervision events, showing the AI autonomously resolved many tasks, supplemented by the physicist's domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach for video diffusion models.
Focuses on optimizing the KV cache by replacing per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent attention (MLA).
Addresses memory and latency issues inherent in long-rollout causal video diffusion models.

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential (time-series) data.
Highlights the need for benchmarks that provide natural-language rationales, moving beyond simple interval annotations.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to enable grounded and interpretable anomaly detection using VLMs.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method, 'sampling from a power distribution,' shows comparable reasoning capabilities to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This sampling technique bypasses the need for extensive post-training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The main practical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately 'mix' into the power distribution.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) for Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a new method designed to improve the efficiency of TTFT by leveraging convex reconstruction and gradient caching.
The research aims to balance the trade-off between speed and quality in prompt adaptation, where existing methods often sacrifice one for the other.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Proposes Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to enhance fairness in Federated Learning.
Addresses limitations of conventional aggregation schemes which use fixed weights for client contributions.
Focuses on improving stability and fairness in distributed, privacy-sensitive model training.

stories.title: When, why, and how do diffusion posterior samplers fail? A finite-sample lens
stories.author: Sara Fridovich-Keil
bullet_points: The paper investigates the failure modes of diffusion posterior samplers, a technique used in imaging inverse problems.
Diffusion models are noted for their strong ability to model complex natural data distributions.
The core issue addressed is the poor downstream effect on the sampled posterior caused by inexact likelihood approximations used for computational tractability.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor research proposals before resource expenditure.
The benchmark is curated from ICLR submissions, providing a real-world dataset for evaluating AI scientific judgment.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new technique, using a 'power distribution' derived from a base language model, can achieve reasoning capabilities comparable to models trained with reinforcement learning.
This method offers a potential simplification by eliminating the need for additional training, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary challenge identified is the practical implementation of efficiently sampling from this derived power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to generalize to unseen preference domains, overcoming the limitations of fixed multi-reward frameworks.
The research focuses on improving the robustness of LLM alignment by dynamically adapting reward models rather than relying on a single, static model.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, where intermediate steps are tied to external token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition to use internal 'working memory' for information manipulation.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from the autoregressive generation process to improve reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
It formalizes this issue using the 'compositional residual eps*', which measures the deviation from a joint coherent polytope.
This metric allows for runtime computation of incoherence based on system output and defined cross-component constraints.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: Introduces 'COMPOSE,' a method for generating plausible future mathematical claims.
The model addresses the limitations of prior work by considering two constraints: following prior research direction and respecting formal dependencies.
This represents a step towards grounded future mathematical generation, aiming for more robust and motivated theoretical claims.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to traditional Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for advanced reasoning models.
It suggests using a 'power distribution' derived from the base model's sampling to achieve comparable reasoning performance without extra training or datasets.
The main technical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this new power distribution.

stories.title: 1-Bit Bonsai Image 4B Image Generation for Local Devices
stories.author: modinfo
bullet_points: Introduces a novel image generation technique capable of producing 4B images from a 1-bit input.
The key feature is the ability to run this generation process locally on consumer devices.
This represents a step toward more accessible, resource-efficient generative AI.

stories.title: Unlawful by design: Exposing the human rights costs of generative AI
stories.author: amnesty.org via mccd
bullet_points: The article critically examines the human rights implications of generative AI.
It argues that certain aspects of generative AI are inherently problematic or 'unlawful by design'.
The piece serves as a commentary on the ethical and societal costs associated with AI development.

stories.title: I Made My AI Models Argue, Then Let Hermes Be the Judge
stories.author: arqamwd
bullet_points: The article details a system where multiple Large Language Models (LLMs) are used to debate a topic.
A specific LLM, named Hermes, is employed to act as the judge or arbiter of the debate.
The core concept involves creating a 'multi-model decision agent' that learns to assess and trust the outputs of the debating models.

stories.title: The Industry Needs an Open Reasoning Spec. Seven Papers Explain What Goes In It.
stories.author: bala_paranj_059d338e44e7e
bullet_points: The article discusses the lack of standardization in reasoning, properties, and contracts within AI-generated code, contrasting it with established API standards.
It highlights that current AI code generation often bypasses the necessary specifications that ensure code safety and modifiability.
Seven foundational Computer Science papers are cited as converging on the necessary components for a robust 'open reasoning specification'.
